The foundational scriptures include the {{ill|Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja|宿曜経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 文殊師利菩薩及諸仙所説吉凶時日善悪宿曜経|1=Sūtra on the Astrology of Auspicious and Inauspicious Days and Good and Evil Spoken by Mañjuśrī Bodhisattva and Celestial Beings|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500028}}, {{ill|Brahma Agni Sutra|ja|梵天火羅九曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Brahma Agni Sutra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Shichi Yō Seishin Betsu Gōhō|ja|七曜星辰別行法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi Yō Seishin Betsu Gōhō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. One unique technique is {{ill|Sankū Hikkyū|ja|三九秘宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankū Hikkyū|1=Sankū Hikkyū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354168}}, which has a simplified version popularized as "Shukuyō astrology."
In [[:en:Mikkyō|Esoteric Buddhism]], selecting auspicious dates for rituals such as statue construction, ceremonies, or consecration was a crucial task. The {{ill|Mahāvairocanābhisaṃbodhi Sūtra Commentary|ja|大日経疏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mahāvairocanābhisaṃbodhi Sūtra Commentary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by [[:en:Yi Xing|Ichigyō]] emphasized the importance of this process as a test of an [[:en:Acharya|Acharya]]'s abilities.
Monks such as [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], [[:en:Ennin|Ennin]], and [[:en:Enchin|Enchin]] brought the {{ill|Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja|宿曜経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 文殊師利菩薩及諸仙所説吉凶時日善悪宿曜経|1=Sūtra on the Astrology of Auspicious and Inauspicious Days and Good and Evil Spoken by Mañjuśrī Bodhisattva and Celestial Beings|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500028}} to Japan, and {{ill|Ninkan|ja|仁観|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} further developed its study. In [[:en:957|957]] ([[:en:Tiande|Tento era]] 1), {{ill|Nichien|ja|日延|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichien|qq=}} brought the {{ill|Futen Calendar|ja|符天暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futen Calendar|qq=}} from [[:en:Wuyue|Wuyue]], intensifying research. {{ill|Hōzō (Japanese monk)|ja|法蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōzō (Japanese monk)|1=Hōzō (Japanese monk)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555339}} in [[:en:Ōwa|Ōwa era]] 1 ([[:en:963|963]]) debated [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]] over the use of auspicious dates for [[:en:Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami]]’s offerings. This marked the formal establishment of Shukuyōdō in Japan.
== Legacy ==
* In the [[:en:The Tale of Genji|Genji Monogatari]], a scene in the "Kiritsubo" chapter describes [[:en:Hikaru Genji|Hikaru Genji]]’s fate being predicted by a Shukuyō astrologer. * During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], [[:en:Kujō Yoritsune|Fujiwara no Yoritsune]] employed a Shukuyō practitioner, {{ill|Chinhyo|ja|珍誉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinhyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, to select auspicious locations for the {{ill|Shogunate|ja|御所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogunate|1=gosho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489051}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Shukuyō astrology|ja|宿曜占星術|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shukuyō astrology|1=Shukuyō astrology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455622}} * [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] * {{ill|Futen Calendar|ja|符天暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futen Calendar|qq=}} * {{ill|Rokumei|ja|禄命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokumei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Festival|qq=}}
== External Links == * {{kotobank|2=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|宿曜道}} * [https://yakumoin.net/宿曜占星術 宿曜占星術 八雲院] * [https://fs-astrology-members.com/宿曜占星術 宿曜占星術+news] * [https://syukuyo.com/宿曜秘宝協会 宿曜秘宝協会] * [https://kosei-do.co.jp/fortune/uranai.php 宿曜占星術 光晴堂]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sukuyōdō]][[ja:宿曜道]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11455623]] Wikidata name: Sukuyodo [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Cite_sources|date=2019-10-07}}
{{nihongo|Shukuyōdō|宿曜道}} is a type of [[:en:Astrology|astrology]] introduced to [[:en:Japan|Japan]] during the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] by monks studying abroad, including [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], as a branch of [[:en:Mikkyō|Esoteric Buddhism]]. It is also known as "Esoteric astrology" or "Shukuyō astrology."
== Overview ==
The practice blends elements of [[:en:Hindu astrology|Indian astrology]] (a fusion of [[:en:Western astrology|Western astrology]] and ancient Indian lunar astrology), Taoist celestial deity worship, and {{ill|Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽五行思想}}. It determines auspicious and inauspicious days based on the movements of celestial bodies such as the [[:en:Big Dipper|Big Dipper]], [[:en:Navagraha|Navagraha]], {{ill|Zodiac|ja|十二宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zodiac|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サイン (占星術)|1=occidental astrological sign|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1795024}}, and lunar mansions like the {{ill|Nakshatra|ja|二十七宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakshatra|1=Nakshatra|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371366}} or [[:en:Twenty-Eight Mansions|Lunar mansions]]. When an inauspicious day is identified, practitioners would perform rituals to honor the deities associated with those stars to improve fortune.
The foundational scriptures include the {{ill|Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja|宿曜経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 文殊師利菩薩及諸仙所説吉凶時日善悪宿曜経|1=Sūtra on the Astrology of Auspicious and Inauspicious Days and Good and Evil Spoken by Mañjuśrī Bodhisattva and Celestial Beings|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500028}}, {{ill|Brahma Agni Sutra|ja|梵天火羅九曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Brahma Agni Sutra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Shichi Yō Seishin Betsu Gōhō|ja|七曜星辰別行法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi Yō Seishin Betsu Gōhō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. One unique technique is {{ill|Sankū Hikkyū|ja|三九秘宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankū Hikkyū|1=Sankū Hikkyū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354168}}, which has a simplified version popularized as "Shukuyō astrology."
In [[:en:Mikkyō|Esoteric Buddhism]], selecting auspicious dates for rituals such as statue construction, ceremonies, or consecration was a crucial task. The {{ill|Mahāvairocanābhisaṃbodhi Sūtra Commentary|ja|大日経疏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mahāvairocanābhisaṃbodhi Sūtra Commentary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by [[:en:Yi Xing|Ichigyō]] emphasized the importance of this process as a test of an [[:en:Acharya|Acharya]]'s abilities.
Monks such as [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], [[:en:Ennin|Ennin]], and [[:en:Enchin|Enchin]] brought the {{ill|Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja|宿曜経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 文殊師利菩薩及諸仙所説吉凶時日善悪宿曜経|1=Sūtra on the Astrology of Auspicious and Inauspicious Days and Good and Evil Spoken by Mañjuśrī Bodhisattva and Celestial Beings|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500028}} to Japan, and {{ill|Ninkan|ja|仁観|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} further developed its study. In [[:en:957|957]] ([[:en:Tiande|Tento era]] 1), {{ill|Nichien|ja|日延|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichien|qq=}} brought the {{ill|Futen Calendar|ja|符天暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futen Calendar|qq=}} from [[:en:Wuyue|Wuyue]], intensifying research. {{ill|Hōzō (Japanese monk)|ja|法蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōzō (Japanese monk)|1=Hōzō (Japanese monk)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555339}} in [[:en:Ōwa|Ōwa era]] 1 ([[:en:963|963]]) debated [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]] over the use of auspicious dates for [[:en:Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami]]’s offerings. This marked the formal establishment of Shukuyōdō in Japan.
== Legacy ==
* In the [[:en:The Tale of Genji|Genji Monogatari]], a scene in the "Kiritsubo" chapter describes [[:en:Hikaru Genji|Hikaru Genji]]’s fate being predicted by a Shukuyō astrologer. * During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], [[:en:Kujō Yoritsune|Fujiwara no Yoritsune]] employed a Shukuyō practitioner, {{ill|Chinhyo|ja|珍誉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinhyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, to select auspicious locations for the {{ill|Shogunate|ja|御所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogunate|1=gosho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489051}}.

The foundational scriptures include the {{ill|Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja|宿曜経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 文殊師利菩薩及諸仙所説吉凶時日善悪宿曜経|1=Sūtra on the Astrology of Auspicious and Inauspicious Days and Good and Evil Spoken by Mañjuśrī Bodhisattva and Celestial Beings|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500028}}, {{ill|Brahma Agni Sutra|ja|梵天火羅九曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Brahma Agni Sutra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Shichi Yō Seishin Betsu Gōhō|ja|七曜星辰別行法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi Yō Seishin Betsu Gōhō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. One unique technique is {{ill|Sankū Hikkyū|ja|三九秘宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankū Hikkyū|1=Sankū Hikkyū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354168}}, which has a simplified version popularized as "Shukuyō astrology."
In [[:en:Mikkyō|Esoteric Buddhism]], selecting auspicious dates for rituals such as statue construction, ceremonies, or consecration was a crucial task. The {{ill|Mahāvairocanābhisaṃbodhi Sūtra Commentary|ja|大日経疏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mahāvairocanābhisaṃbodhi Sūtra Commentary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by [[:en:Yi Xing|Ichigyō]] emphasized the importance of this process as a test of an [[:en:Acharya|Acharya]]'s abilities.
Monks such as [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], [[:en:Ennin|Ennin]], and [[:en:Enchin|Enchin]] brought the {{ill|Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja|宿曜経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 文殊師利菩薩及諸仙所説吉凶時日善悪宿曜経|1=Sūtra on the Astrology of Auspicious and Inauspicious Days and Good and Evil Spoken by Mañjuśrī Bodhisattva and Celestial Beings|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500028}} to Japan, and {{ill|Ninkan|ja|仁観|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} further developed its study. In [[:en:957|957]] ([[:en:Tiande|Tento era]] 1), {{ill|Nichien|ja|日延|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichien|qq=}} brought the {{ill|Futen Calendar|ja|符天暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futen Calendar|qq=}} from [[:en:Wuyue|Wuyue]], intensifying research. {{ill|Hōzō (Japanese monk)|ja|法蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōzō (Japanese monk)|1=Hōzō (Japanese monk)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555339}} in [[:en:Ōwa|Ōwa era]] 1 ([[:en:963|963]]) debated [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]] over the use of auspicious dates for [[:en:Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami]]’s offerings. This marked the formal establishment of Shukuyōdō in Japan.
== Legacy ==
* In the [[:en:The Tale of Genji|Genji Monogatari]], a scene in the "Kiritsubo" chapter describes [[:en:Hikaru Genji|Hikaru Genji]]’s fate being predicted by a Shukuyō astrologer. * During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], [[:en:Kujō Yoritsune|Fujiwara no Yoritsune]] employed a Shukuyō practitioner, {{ill|Chinhyo|ja|珍誉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinhyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, to select auspicious locations for the {{ill|Shogunate|ja|御所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogunate|1=gosho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489051}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Sukuyōdō]][[ja:宿曜道]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Sukuyōdō]][[ja:宿曜道]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sukuyōdō]][[ja:宿曜道]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11455623]] Wikidata name: Sukuyodo [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Cite_sources|date=2019-10-07}}
{{nihongo|Shukuyōdō|宿曜道}} is a type of [[:en:Astrology|astrology]] introduced to [[:en:Japan|Japan]] during the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] by monks studying abroad, including [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], as a branch of [[:en:Mikkyō|Esoteric Buddhism]]. It is also known as "Esoteric astrology" or "Shukuyō astrology."
== Overview ==
The practice blends elements of [[:en:Hindu astrology|Indian astrology]] (a fusion of [[:en:Western astrology|Western astrology]] and ancient Indian lunar astrology), Taoist celestial deity worship, and {{ill|Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽五行思想}}. It determines auspicious and inauspicious days based on the movements of celestial bodies such as the [[:en:Big Dipper|Big Dipper]], [[:en:Navagraha|Navagraha]], {{ill|Zodiac|ja|十二宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zodiac|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サイン (占星術)|1=occidental astrological sign|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1795024}}, and lunar mansions like the {{ill|Nakshatra|ja|二十七宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakshatra|1=Nakshatra|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371366}} or [[:en:Twenty-Eight Mansions|Lunar mansions]]. When an inauspicious day is identified, practitioners would perform rituals to honor the deities associated with those stars to improve fortune.
The foundational scriptures include the {{ill|Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja|宿曜経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 文殊師利菩薩及諸仙所説吉凶時日善悪宿曜経|1=Sūtra on the Astrology of Auspicious and Inauspicious Days and Good and Evil Spoken by Mañjuśrī Bodhisattva and Celestial Beings|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500028}}, {{ill|Brahma Agni Sutra|ja|梵天火羅九曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Brahma Agni Sutra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Shichi Yō Seishin Betsu Gōhō|ja|七曜星辰別行法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi Yō Seishin Betsu Gōhō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. One unique technique is {{ill|Sankū Hikkyū|ja|三九秘宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankū Hikkyū|1=Sankū Hikkyū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354168}}, which has a simplified version popularized as "Shukuyō astrology."
In [[:en:Mikkyō|Esoteric Buddhism]], selecting auspicious dates for rituals such as statue construction, ceremonies, or consecration was a crucial task. The {{ill|Mahāvairocanābhisaṃbodhi Sūtra Commentary|ja|大日経疏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mahāvairocanābhisaṃbodhi Sūtra Commentary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by [[:en:Yi Xing|Ichigyō]] emphasized the importance of this process as a test of an [[:en:Acharya|Acharya]]'s abilities.
Monks such as [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], [[:en:Ennin|Ennin]], and [[:en:Enchin|Enchin]] brought the {{ill|Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja|宿曜経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 文殊師利菩薩及諸仙所説吉凶時日善悪宿曜経|1=Sūtra on the Astrology of Auspicious and Inauspicious Days and Good and Evil Spoken by Mañjuśrī Bodhisattva and Celestial Beings|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500028}} to Japan, and {{ill|Ninkan|ja|仁観|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} further developed its study. In [[:en:957|957]] ([[:en:Tiande|Tento era]] 1), {{ill|Nichien|ja|日延|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichien|qq=}} brought the {{ill|Futen Calendar|ja|符天暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futen Calendar|qq=}} from [[:en:Wuyue|Wuyue]], intensifying research. {{ill|Hōzō (Japanese monk)|ja|法蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōzō (Japanese monk)|1=Hōzō (Japanese monk)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555339}} in [[:en:Ōwa|Ōwa era]] 1 ([[:en:963|963]]) debated [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]] over the use of auspicious dates for [[:en:Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami]]’s offerings. This marked the formal establishment of Shukuyōdō in Japan.
== Legacy ==
* In the [[:en:The Tale of Genji|Genji Monogatari]], a scene in the "Kiritsubo" chapter describes [[:en:Hikaru Genji|Hikaru Genji]]’s fate being predicted by a Shukuyō astrologer. * During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], [[:en:Kujō Yoritsune|Fujiwara no Yoritsune]] employed a Shukuyō practitioner, {{ill|Chinhyo|ja|珍誉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinhyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, to select auspicious locations for the {{ill|Shogunate|ja|御所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogunate|1=gosho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489051}}.

The foundational scriptures include the {{ill|Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja|宿曜経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 文殊師利菩薩及諸仙所説吉凶時日善悪宿曜経|1=Sūtra on the Astrology of Auspicious and Inauspicious Days and Good and Evil Spoken by Mañjuśrī Bodhisattva and Celestial Beings|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500028}}, {{ill|Brahma Agni Sutra|ja|梵天火羅九曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Brahma Agni Sutra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Shichi Yō Seishin Betsu Gōhō|ja|七曜星辰別行法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi Yō Seishin Betsu Gōhō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. One unique technique is {{ill|Sankū Hikkyū|ja|三九秘宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankū Hikkyū|1=Sankū Hikkyū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354168}}, which has a simplified version popularized as "Shukuyō astrology."
In [[:en:Mikkyō|Esoteric Buddhism]], selecting auspicious dates for rituals such as statue construction, ceremonies, or consecration was a crucial task. The {{ill|Mahāvairocanābhisaṃbodhi Sūtra Commentary|ja|大日経疏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mahāvairocanābhisaṃbodhi Sūtra Commentary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by [[:en:Yi Xing|Ichigyō]] emphasized the importance of this process as a test of an [[:en:Acharya|Acharya]]'s abilities.
Monks such as [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], [[:en:Ennin|Ennin]], and [[:en:Enchin|Enchin]] brought the {{ill|Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja|宿曜経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 文殊師利菩薩及諸仙所説吉凶時日善悪宿曜経|1=Sūtra on the Astrology of Auspicious and Inauspicious Days and Good and Evil Spoken by Mañjuśrī Bodhisattva and Celestial Beings|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500028}} to Japan, and {{ill|Ninkan|ja|仁観|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} further developed its study. In [[:en:957|957]] ([[:en:Tiande|Tento era]] 1), {{ill|Nichien|ja|日延|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichien|qq=}} brought the {{ill|Futen Calendar|ja|符天暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futen Calendar|qq=}} from [[:en:Wuyue|Wuyue]], intensifying research. {{ill|Hōzō (Japanese monk)|ja|法蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōzō (Japanese monk)|1=Hōzō (Japanese monk)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555339}} in [[:en:Ōwa|Ōwa era]] 1 ([[:en:963|963]]) debated [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]] over the use of auspicious dates for [[:en:Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami]]’s offerings. This marked the formal establishment of Shukuyōdō in Japan.
== Legacy ==
* In the [[:en:The Tale of Genji|Genji Monogatari]], a scene in the "Kiritsubo" chapter describes [[:en:Hikaru Genji|Hikaru Genji]]’s fate being predicted by a Shukuyō astrologer. * During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], [[:en:Kujō Yoritsune|Fujiwara no Yoritsune]] employed a Shukuyō practitioner, {{ill|Chinhyo|ja|珍誉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinhyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, to select auspicious locations for the {{ill|Shogunate|ja|御所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogunate|1=gosho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489051}}.

{{nihongo|Shukuyōdō|宿曜道}} is a type of [[:en:Astrology|astrology]] introduced to [[:en:Japan|Japan]] during the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] by monks studying abroad, including [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], as a branch of [[:en:Mikkyō|Esoteric Buddhism]]. It is also known as "Esoteric astrology" or "Shukuyō astrology."
== Overview ==
The practice blends elements of [[:en:Hindu astrology|Indian astrology]] (a fusion of [[:en:Western astrology|Western astrology]] and ancient Indian lunar astrology), Taoist celestial deity worship, and {{ill|Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽五行思想}}. It determines auspicious and inauspicious days based on the movements of celestial bodies such as the [[:en:Big Dipper|Big Dipper]], [[:en:Navagraha|Navagraha]], {{ill|Zodiac|ja|十二宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zodiac|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サイン (占星術)|1=occidental astrological sign|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1795024}}, and lunar mansions like the {{ill|Nakshatra|ja|二十七宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakshatra|1=Nakshatra|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371366}} or [[:en:Twenty-Eight Mansions|Lunar mansions]]. When an inauspicious day is identified, practitioners would perform rituals to honor the deities associated with those stars to improve fortune.
The foundational scriptures include the {{ill|Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja|宿曜経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 文殊師利菩薩及諸仙所説吉凶時日善悪宿曜経|1=Sūtra on the Astrology of Auspicious and Inauspicious Days and Good and Evil Spoken by Mañjuśrī Bodhisattva and Celestial Beings|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500028}}, {{ill|Brahma Agni Sutra|ja|梵天火羅九曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Brahma Agni Sutra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Shichi Yō Seishin Betsu Gōhō|ja|七曜星辰別行法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi Yō Seishin Betsu Gōhō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. One unique technique is {{ill|Sankū Hikkyū|ja|三九秘宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankū Hikkyū|1=Sankū Hikkyū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354168}}, which has a simplified version popularized as "Shukuyō astrology."
In [[:en:Mikkyō|Esoteric Buddhism]], selecting auspicious dates for rituals such as statue construction, ceremonies, or consecration was a crucial task. The {{ill|Mahāvairocanābhisaṃbodhi Sūtra Commentary|ja|大日経疏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mahāvairocanābhisaṃbodhi Sūtra Commentary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by [[:en:Yi Xing|Ichigyō]] emphasized the importance of this process as a test of an [[:en:Acharya|Acharya]]'s abilities.
Monks such as [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], [[:en:Ennin|Ennin]], and [[:en:Enchin|Enchin]] brought the {{ill|Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja|宿曜経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 文殊師利菩薩及諸仙所説吉凶時日善悪宿曜経|1=Sūtra on the Astrology of Auspicious and Inauspicious Days and Good and Evil Spoken by Mañjuśrī Bodhisattva and Celestial Beings|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500028}} to Japan, and {{ill|Ninkan|ja|仁観|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} further developed its study. In [[:en:957|957]] ([[:en:Tiande|Tento era]] 1), {{ill|Nichien|ja|日延|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichien|qq=}} brought the {{ill|Futen Calendar|ja|符天暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futen Calendar|qq=}} from [[:en:Wuyue|Wuyue]], intensifying research. {{ill|Hōzō (Japanese monk)|ja|法蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōzō (Japanese monk)|1=Hōzō (Japanese monk)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555339}} in [[:en:Ōwa|Ōwa era]] 1 ([[:en:963|963]]) debated [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]] over the use of auspicious dates for [[:en:Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami]]’s offerings. This marked the formal establishment of Shukuyōdō in Japan.
== Legacy ==
* In the [[:en:The Tale of Genji|Genji Monogatari]], a scene in the "Kiritsubo" chapter describes [[:en:Hikaru Genji|Hikaru Genji]]’s fate being predicted by a Shukuyō astrologer. * During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], [[:en:Kujō Yoritsune|Fujiwara no Yoritsune]] employed a Shukuyō practitioner, {{ill|Chinhyo|ja|珍誉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinhyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, to select auspicious locations for the {{ill|Shogunate|ja|御所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogunate|1=gosho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489051}}.

It determines auspicious and inauspicious days based on the movements of celestial bodies such as the [[:en:Big Dipper|Big Dipper]], [[:en:Navagraha|Navagraha]], {{ill|Zodiac|ja|十二宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zodiac|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サイン (占星術)|1=occidental astrological sign|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1795024}}, and lunar mansions like the {{ill|Nakshatra|ja|二十七宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakshatra|1=Nakshatra|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371366}} or [[:en:Twenty-Eight Mansions|Lunar mansions]]. When an inauspicious day is identified, practitioners would perform rituals to honor the deities associated with those stars to improve fortune.
The foundational scriptures include the {{ill|Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja|宿曜経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 文殊師利菩薩及諸仙所説吉凶時日善悪宿曜経|1=Sūtra on the Astrology of Auspicious and Inauspicious Days and Good and Evil Spoken by Mañjuśrī Bodhisattva and Celestial Beings|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500028}}, {{ill|Brahma Agni Sutra|ja|梵天火羅九曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Brahma Agni Sutra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Shichi Yō Seishin Betsu Gōhō|ja|七曜星辰別行法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi Yō Seishin Betsu Gōhō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. One unique technique is {{ill|Sankū Hikkyū|ja|三九秘宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankū Hikkyū|1=Sankū Hikkyū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354168}}, which has a simplified version popularized as "Shukuyō astrology."
In [[:en:Mikkyō|Esoteric Buddhism]], selecting auspicious dates for rituals such as statue construction, ceremonies, or consecration was a crucial task. The {{ill|Mahāvairocanābhisaṃbodhi Sūtra Commentary|ja|大日経疏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mahāvairocanābhisaṃbodhi Sūtra Commentary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by [[:en:Yi Xing|Ichigyō]] emphasized the importance of this process as a test of an [[:en:Acharya|Acharya]]'s abilities.
Monks such as [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], [[:en:Ennin|Ennin]], and [[:en:Enchin|Enchin]] brought the {{ill|Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja|宿曜経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 文殊師利菩薩及諸仙所説吉凶時日善悪宿曜経|1=Sūtra on the Astrology of Auspicious and Inauspicious Days and Good and Evil Spoken by Mañjuśrī Bodhisattva and Celestial Beings|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500028}} to Japan, and {{ill|Ninkan|ja|仁観|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} further developed its study. In [[:en:957|957]] ([[:en:Tiande|Tento era]] 1), {{ill|Nichien|ja|日延|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichien|qq=}} brought the {{ill|Futen Calendar|ja|符天暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futen Calendar|qq=}} from [[:en:Wuyue|Wuyue]], intensifying research. {{ill|Hōzō (Japanese monk)|ja|法蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōzō (Japanese monk)|1=Hōzō (Japanese monk)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555339}} in [[:en:Ōwa|Ōwa era]] 1 ([[:en:963|963]]) debated [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]] over the use of auspicious dates for [[:en:Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami]]’s offerings. This marked the formal establishment of Shukuyōdō in Japan.
== Legacy ==
* In the [[:en:The Tale of Genji|Genji Monogatari]], a scene in the "Kiritsubo" chapter describes [[:en:Hikaru Genji|Hikaru Genji]]’s fate being predicted by a Shukuyō astrologer. * During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], [[:en:Kujō Yoritsune|Fujiwara no Yoritsune]] employed a Shukuyō practitioner, {{ill|Chinhyo|ja|珍誉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinhyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, to select auspicious locations for the {{ill|Shogunate|ja|御所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogunate|1=gosho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489051}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Shukuyō astrology|ja|宿曜占星術|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shukuyō astrology|1=Shukuyō astrology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455622}} * [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] * {{ill|Futen Calendar|ja|符天暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futen Calendar|qq=}} * {{ill|Rokumei|ja|禄命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokumei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Festival|qq=}}
== External Links == * {{kotobank|2=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|宿曜道}} * [https://yakumoin.net/宿曜占星術 宿曜占星術 八雲院] * [https://fs-astrology-members.com/宿曜占星術 宿曜占星術+news] * [https://syukuyo.com/宿曜秘宝協会 宿曜秘宝協会] * [https://kosei-do.co.jp/fortune/uranai.php 宿曜占星術 光晴堂]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sukuyōdō]][[ja:宿曜道]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sukuyōdō]][[ja:宿曜道]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sukuyōdō]][[ja:宿曜道]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11455623]] Wikidata name: Sukuyodo [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Cite_sources|date=2019-10-07}}
{{nihongo|Shukuyōdō|宿曜道}} is a type of {{ill|Astrology|en|Astrology|WD=Q34362|lt=astrology}} introduced to {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} by monks studying abroad, including {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|WD=Q222227|lt=Kūkai}}, as a branch of {{ill|Mikkyō|en|Mikkyō|WD=Q336102|lt=Esoteric Buddhism}}. It is also known as "Esoteric astrology" or "Shukuyō astrology."
== Overview ==
The practice blends elements of {{ill|Hindu astrology|en|Hindu astrology|WD=Q740253|lt=Indian astrology}} (a fusion of {{ill|Western astrology|en|Western astrology|WD=Q2266871|lt=Western astrology}} and ancient Indian lunar astrology), Taoist celestial deity worship, and {{ill|Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽五行思想}}. It determines auspicious and inauspicious days based on the movements of celestial bodies such as the {{ill|Big Dipper|en|Big Dipper|WD=Q10460|lt=Big Dipper}}, {{ill|Navagraha|en|Navagraha|WD=Q1078632|lt=Navagraha}}, {{ill|Zodiac|ja|十二宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zodiac|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サイン (占星術)|1=occidental astrological sign|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1795024}}, and lunar mansions like the {{ill|Nakshatra|ja|二十七宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakshatra|1=Nakshatra|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371366}} or {{ill|Twenty-Eight Mansions|en|Twenty-Eight Mansions|WD=Q1378760|lt=Lunar mansions}}. When an inauspicious day is identified, practitioners would perform rituals to honor the deities associated with those stars to improve fortune.
The foundational scriptures include the {{ill|Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja|宿曜経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 文殊師利菩薩及諸仙所説吉凶時日善悪宿曜経|1=Sūtra on the Astrology of Auspicious and Inauspicious Days and Good and Evil Spoken by Mañjuśrī Bodhisattva and Celestial Beings|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500028}}, {{ill|Brahma Agni Sutra|ja|梵天火羅九曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Brahma Agni Sutra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Shichi Yō Seishin Betsu Gōhō|ja|七曜星辰別行法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi Yō Seishin Betsu Gōhō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. One unique technique is {{ill|Sankū Hikkyū|ja|三九秘宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankū Hikkyū|1=Sankū Hikkyū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354168}}, which has a simplified version popularized as "Shukuyō astrology."
In {{ill|Mikkyō|en|Mikkyō|WD=Q336102|lt=Esoteric Buddhism}}, selecting auspicious dates for rituals such as statue construction, ceremonies, or consecration was a crucial task. The {{ill|Mahāvairocanābhisaṃbodhi Sūtra Commentary|ja|大日経疏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mahāvairocanābhisaṃbodhi Sūtra Commentary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by {{ill|Yi Xing|en|Yi Xing|WD=Q710705|lt=Ichigyō}} emphasized the importance of this process as a test of an {{ill|Acharya|en|Acharya|WD=Q1351823|lt=Acharya}}'s abilities.
Monks such as {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|WD=Q222227|lt=Kūkai}}, {{ill|Ennin|en|Ennin|WD=Q732282|lt=Ennin}}, and {{ill|Enchin|en|Enchin|WD=Q1972505|lt=Enchin}} brought the {{ill|Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja|宿曜経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 文殊師利菩薩及諸仙所説吉凶時日善悪宿曜経|1=Sūtra on the Astrology of Auspicious and Inauspicious Days and Good and Evil Spoken by Mañjuśrī Bodhisattva and Celestial Beings|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500028}} to Japan, and {{ill|Ninkan|ja|仁観|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} further developed its study. In {{ill|957|en|957|WD=Q23711|lt=957}} ({{ill|Tiande|en|Tiande|WD=Q10939590|lt=Tento era}} 1), {{ill|Nichien|ja|日延|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichien|qq=}} brought the {{ill|Futen Calendar|ja|符天暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futen Calendar|qq=}} from {{ill|Wuyue|en|Wuyue|WD=Q1193822|lt=Wuyue}}, intensifying research. {{ill|Hōzō (Japanese monk)|ja|法蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōzō (Japanese monk)|1=Hōzō (Japanese monk)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555339}} in {{ill|Ōwa|en|Ōwa|WD=Q1324768|lt=Ōwa era}} 1 ({{ill|963|en|963|WD=Q23562|lt=963}}) debated {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|en|Kamo no Yasunori|WD=Q6358935|lt=Kamo no Yasunori}} over the use of auspicious dates for {{ill|Emperor Murakami|en|Emperor Murakami|WD=Q374015|lt=Emperor Murakami}}’s offerings. This marked the formal establishment of Shukuyōdō in Japan.
== Legacy ==
* In the {{ill|The Tale of Genji|en|The Tale of Genji|WD=Q8269|lt=Genji Monogatari}}, a scene in the "Kiritsubo" chapter describes {{ill|Hikaru Genji|en|Hikaru Genji|WD=Q997646|lt=Hikaru Genji}}’s fate being predicted by a Shukuyō astrologer. * During the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, {{ill|Kujō Yoritsune|en|Kujō Yoritsune|WD=Q471780|lt=Fujiwara no Yoritsune}} employed a Shukuyō practitioner, {{ill|Chinhyo|ja|珍誉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinhyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, to select auspicious locations for the {{ill|Shogunate|ja|御所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogunate|1=gosho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489051}}.

== Related Articles == * {{ill|Shukuyō astrology|ja|宿曜占星術|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shukuyō astrology|1=Shukuyō astrology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455622}} * {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō}} * {{ill|Futen Calendar|ja|符天暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futen Calendar|qq=}} * {{ill|Rokumei|ja|禄命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokumei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Festival|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sukuyōdō]][[ja:宿曜道]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sukuyōdō]][[ja:宿曜道]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sukuyōdō]][[ja:宿曜道]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11455623]] Wikidata name: Sukuyodo [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Cite_sources|date=2019-10-07}}
{{nihongo|Shukuyōdō|宿曜道}} is a type of {{ill|Astrology|en|Astrology|WD=Q34362|lt=astrology}} introduced to {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} by monks studying abroad, including {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|WD=Q222227|lt=Kūkai}}, as a branch of {{ill|Mikkyō|en|Mikkyō|WD=Q336102|lt=Esoteric Buddhism}}. It is also known as "Esoteric astrology" or "Shukuyō astrology."
== Overview ==
The practice blends elements of {{ill|Hindu astrology|en|Hindu astrology|WD=Q740253|lt=Indian astrology}} (a fusion of {{ill|Western astrology|en|Western astrology|WD=Q2266871|lt=Western astrology}} and ancient Indian lunar astrology), Taoist celestial deity worship, and {{ill|Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽五行思想}}. It determines auspicious and inauspicious days based on the movements of celestial bodies such as the {{ill|Big Dipper|en|Big Dipper|WD=Q10460|lt=Big Dipper}}, {{ill|Navagraha|en|Navagraha|WD=Q1078632|lt=Navagraha}}, {{ill|Zodiac|ja|十二宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zodiac|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サイン (占星術)|1=occidental astrological sign|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1795024}}, and lunar mansions like the {{ill|Nakshatra|ja|二十七宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakshatra|1=Nakshatra|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371366}} or {{ill|Twenty-Eight Mansions|en|Twenty-Eight Mansions|WD=Q1378760|lt=Lunar mansions}}. When an inauspicious day is identified, practitioners would perform rituals to honor the deities associated with those stars to improve fortune.
The foundational scriptures include the {{ill|Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja|宿曜経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 文殊師利菩薩及諸仙所説吉凶時日善悪宿曜経|1=Sūtra on the Astrology of Auspicious and Inauspicious Days and Good and Evil Spoken by Mañjuśrī Bodhisattva and Celestial Beings|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500028}}, {{ill|Brahma Agni Sutra|ja|梵天火羅九曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Brahma Agni Sutra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Shichi Yō Seishin Betsu Gōhō|ja|七曜星辰別行法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi Yō Seishin Betsu Gōhō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. One unique technique is {{ill|Sankū Hikkyū|ja|三九秘宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankū Hikkyū|1=Sankū Hikkyū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354168}}, which has a simplified version popularized as "Shukuyō astrology."
In {{ill|Mikkyō|en|Mikkyō|WD=Q336102|lt=Esoteric Buddhism}}, selecting auspicious dates for rituals such as statue construction, ceremonies, or consecration was a crucial task. The {{ill|Mahāvairocanābhisaṃbodhi Sūtra Commentary|ja|大日経疏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mahāvairocanābhisaṃbodhi Sūtra Commentary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by {{ill|Yi Xing|en|Yi Xing|WD=Q710705|lt=Ichigyō}} emphasized the importance of this process as a test of an {{ill|Acharya|en|Acharya|WD=Q1351823|lt=Acharya}}'s abilities.
Monks such as {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|WD=Q222227|lt=Kūkai}}, {{ill|Ennin|en|Ennin|WD=Q732282|lt=Ennin}}, and {{ill|Enchin|en|Enchin|WD=Q1972505|lt=Enchin}} brought the {{ill|Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja|宿曜経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 文殊師利菩薩及諸仙所説吉凶時日善悪宿曜経|1=Sūtra on the Astrology of Auspicious and Inauspicious Days and Good and Evil Spoken by Mañjuśrī Bodhisattva and Celestial Beings|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500028}} to Japan, and {{ill|Ninkan|ja|仁観|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} further developed its study. In {{ill|957|en|957|WD=Q23711|lt=957}} ({{ill|Tiande|en|Tiande|WD=Q10939590|lt=Tento era}} 1), {{ill|Nichien|ja|日延|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichien|qq=}} brought the {{ill|Futen Calendar|ja|符天暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futen Calendar|qq=}} from {{ill|Wuyue|en|Wuyue|WD=Q1193822|lt=Wuyue}}, intensifying research. {{ill|Hōzō (Japanese monk)|ja|法蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōzō (Japanese monk)|1=Hōzō (Japanese monk)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555339}} in {{ill|Ōwa|en|Ōwa|WD=Q1324768|lt=Ōwa era}} 1 ({{ill|963|en|963|WD=Q23562|lt=963}}) debated {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|en|Kamo no Yasunori|WD=Q6358935|lt=Kamo no Yasunori}} over the use of auspicious dates for {{ill|Emperor Murakami|en|Emperor Murakami|WD=Q374015|lt=Emperor Murakami}}’s offerings. This marked the formal establishment of Shukuyōdō in Japan.
== Legacy ==
* In the {{ill|The Tale of Genji|en|The Tale of Genji|WD=Q8269|lt=Genji Monogatari}}, a scene in the "Kiritsubo" chapter describes {{ill|Hikaru Genji|en|Hikaru Genji|WD=Q997646|lt=Hikaru Genji}}’s fate being predicted by a Shukuyō astrologer. * During the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, {{ill|Kujō Yoritsune|en|Kujō Yoritsune|WD=Q471780|lt=Fujiwara no Yoritsune}} employed a Shukuyō practitioner, {{ill|Chinhyo|ja|珍誉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinhyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, to select auspicious locations for the {{ill|Shogunate|ja|御所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogunate|1=gosho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489051}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Shukuyō astrology|ja|宿曜占星術|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shukuyō astrology|1=Shukuyō astrology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455622}} * {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō}} * {{ill|Futen Calendar|ja|符天暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futen Calendar|qq=}} * {{ill|Rokumei|ja|禄命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokumei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Festival|qq=}}
== External Links == * {{kotobank|2=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|宿曜道}} * [https://yakumoin.net/宿曜占星術 宿曜占星術 八雲院] * [https://fs-astrology-members.com/宿曜占星術 宿曜占星術+news] * [https://syukuyo.com/宿曜秘宝協会 宿曜秘宝協会] * [https://kosei-do.co.jp/fortune/uranai.php 宿曜占星術 光晴堂]
{{translated page|ja|宿曜道|version=97447275|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:宿曜道]]

This marked the formal establishment of Shukuyōdō in Japan.

* In the {{ill|The Tale of Genji|en|The Tale of Genji|WD=Q8269|lt=Genji Monogatari}}, a scene in the "Kiritsubo" chapter describes {{ill|Hikaru Genji|en|Hikaru Genji|WD=Q997646|lt=Hikaru Genji}}’s fate being predicted by a Shukuyō astrologer. * During the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, {{ill|Kujō Yoritsune|en|Kujō Yoritsune|WD=Q471780|lt=Fujiwara no Yoritsune}} employed a Shukuyō practitioner, {{ill|Chinhyo|ja|珍誉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinhyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, to select auspicious locations for the {{ill|Shogunate|ja|御所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogunate|1=gosho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489051}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Shukuyō astrology|ja|宿曜占星術|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shukuyō astrology|1=Shukuyō astrology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455622}} * {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō}} * {{ill|Futen Calendar|ja|符天暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futen Calendar|qq=}} * {{ill|Rokumei|ja|禄命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokumei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Festival|qq=}}

<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Sukuyōdō}} {{wikidata link|Q11455623}} [[Category:Astrology]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Days of the week]] [[Category:Esoteric Buddhism]] [[Category:History of astronomy in Japan]] [[Category:Sukuyōdō]] [[Category:Vajrayana]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sukuyōdō]][[ja:宿曜道]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sukuyōdō]][[ja:宿曜道]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sukuyōdō]][[ja:宿曜道]]
{{Cite_sources|date=2019-10-07}}
{{nihongo|Shukuyōdō|宿曜道}} is a type of {{ill|Astrology|en|Astrology|WD=Q34362|lt=astrology|1=Astrology}} introduced to {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} by monks studying abroad, including {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|WD=Q222227|lt=Kūkai|1=Kūkai}}, as a branch of {{ill|Mikkyō|en|Mikkyō|WD=Q336102|lt=Esoteric Buddhism|1=Mikkyō}}. It is also known as "Esoteric astrology" or "Shukuyō astrology."
== Overview ==
The practice blends elements of {{ill|Hindu astrology|en|Hindu astrology|WD=Q740253|lt=Indian astrology|1=Hindu astrology}} (a fusion of {{ill|Western astrology|en|Western astrology|WD=Q2266871|lt=Western astrology|1=Western astrology}} and ancient Indian lunar astrology), Taoist celestial deity worship, and {{ill|Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽五行思想}}. It determines auspicious and inauspicious days based on the movements of celestial bodies such as the {{ill|Big Dipper|en|Big Dipper|WD=Q10460|lt=Big Dipper|1=Big Dipper}}, {{ill|Navagraha|en|Navagraha|WD=Q1078632|lt=Navagraha|1=Navagraha}}, {{ill|Zodiac|ja|十二宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zodiac|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サイン (占星術)|1=Astrological sign|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1795024}}, and lunar mansions like the {{ill|Nakshatra|ja|二十七宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakshatra|1=Q11371366|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371366}} or {{ill|Twenty-Eight Mansions|en|Twenty-Eight Mansions|WD=Q1378760|lt=Lunar mansions|1=Twenty-Eight Mansions}}. When an inauspicious day is identified, practitioners would perform rituals to honor the deities associated with those stars to improve fortune.
The foundational scriptures include the {{ill|Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja|宿曜経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 文殊師利菩薩及諸仙所説吉凶時日善悪宿曜経|1=Sūtra on the Astrology of Auspicious and Inauspicious Days and Good and Evil Spoken by Mañjuśrī Bodhisattva and Celestial Beings|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500028}}, {{ill|Brahma Agni Sutra|ja|梵天火羅九曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Brahma Agni Sutra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Shichi Yō Seishin Betsu Gōhō|ja|七曜星辰別行法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi Yō Seishin Betsu Gōhō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. One unique technique is {{ill|Sankū Hikkyū|ja|三九秘宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankū Hikkyū|1=Q11354168|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354168}}, which has a simplified version popularized as "Shukuyō astrology."
In {{ill|Mikkyō|en|Mikkyō|WD=Q336102|lt=Esoteric Buddhism|1=Mikkyō}}, selecting auspicious dates for rituals such as statue construction, ceremonies, or consecration was a crucial task. The {{ill|Mahāvairocanābhisaṃbodhi Sūtra Commentary|ja|大日経疏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mahāvairocanābhisaṃbodhi Sūtra Commentary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by {{ill|Yi Xing|en|Yi Xing|WD=Q710705|lt=Ichigyō|1=Yi Xing}} emphasized the importance of this process as a test of an {{ill|Acharya|en|Acharya|WD=Q1351823|lt=Acharya|1=Acharya}}'s abilities.
Monks such as {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|WD=Q222227|lt=Kūkai|1=Kūkai}}, {{ill|Ennin|en|Ennin|WD=Q732282|lt=Ennin|1=Ennin}}, and {{ill|Enchin|en|Enchin|WD=Q1972505|lt=Enchin|1=Enchin}} brought the {{ill|Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja|宿曜経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 文殊師利菩薩及諸仙所説吉凶時日善悪宿曜経|1=Sūtra on the Astrology of Auspicious and Inauspicious Days and Good and Evil Spoken by Mañjuśrī Bodhisattva and Celestial Beings|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500028}} to Japan, and {{ill|Ninkan|ja|仁観|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} further developed its study. In {{ill|957|en|957|WD=Q23711|lt=957|1=957}} ({{ill|Tiande|en|Tiande|WD=Q10939590|lt=Tento era|1=Tiande}} 1), {{ill|Nichien|ja|日延|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichien|qq=}} brought the {{ill|Futen Calendar|ja|符天暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futen Calendar|qq=}} from {{ill|Wuyue|en|Wuyue|WD=Q1193822|lt=Wuyue|1=Wuyue}}, intensifying research. {{ill|Hōzō (Japanese monk)|ja|法蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōzō (Japanese monk)|1=Q11555339|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555339}} in {{ill|Ōwa|en|Ōwa|WD=Q1324768|lt=Ōwa era|1=Ōwa}} 1 ({{ill|963|en|963|WD=Q23562|lt=963|1=963}}) debated {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|en|Kamo no Yasunori|WD=Q6358935|lt=Kamo no Yasunori|1=Kamo no Yasunori}} over the use of auspicious dates for {{ill|Emperor Murakami|en|Emperor Murakami|WD=Q374015|lt=Emperor Murakami|1=Emperor Murakami}}’s offerings. This marked the formal establishment of Shukuyōdō in Japan.
== Legacy ==
* In the {{ill|The Tale of Genji|en|The Tale of Genji|WD=Q8269|lt=Genji Monogatari|1=The Tale of Genji}}, a scene in the "Kiritsubo" chapter describes {{ill|Hikaru Genji|en|Hikaru Genji|WD=Q997646|lt=Hikaru Genji|1=Hikaru Genji}}’s fate being predicted by a Shukuyō astrologer. * During the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, {{ill|Kujō Yoritsune|en|Kujō Yoritsune|WD=Q471780|lt=Fujiwara no Yoritsune|1=Kujō Yoritsune}} employed a Shukuyō practitioner, {{ill|Chinhyo|ja|珍誉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinhyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, to select auspicious locations for the {{ill|Shogunate|ja|御所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogunate|1=gosho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489051}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Shukuyō astrology|ja|宿曜占星術|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shukuyō astrology|1=Q11455622|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455622}} * {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō|1=Onmyōdō}} * {{ill|Futen Calendar|ja|符天暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futen Calendar|qq=}} * {{ill|Rokumei|ja|禄命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokumei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Festival|qq=}}
== External Links == * {{kotobank|2=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|宿曜道}} * [https://yakumoin.net/宿曜占星術 宿曜占星術 八雲院] * [https://fs-astrology-members.com/宿曜占星術 宿曜占星術+news] * [https://syukuyo.com/宿曜秘宝協会 宿曜秘宝協会] * [https://kosei-do.co.jp/fortune/uranai.php 宿曜占星術 光晴堂]
{{translated page|ja|宿曜道|version=97447275|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The foundational scriptures include the {{ill|Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja|宿曜経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 文殊師利菩薩及諸仙所説吉凶時日善悪宿曜経|1=Sūtra on the Astrology of Auspicious and Inauspicious Days and Good and Evil Spoken by Mañjuśrī Bodhisattva and Celestial Beings|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500028}}, {{ill|Brahma Agni Sutra|ja|梵天火羅九曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Brahma Agni Sutra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Shichi Yō Seishin Betsu Gōhō|ja|七曜星辰別行法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi Yō Seishin Betsu Gōhō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. One unique technique is {{ill|Sankū Hikkyū|ja|三九秘宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankū Hikkyū|1=Q11354168|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354168}}, which has a simplified version popularized as "Shukuyō astrology."
In {{ill|Mikkyō|en|Mikkyō|WD=Q336102|lt=Esoteric Buddhism|1=Mikkyō}}, selecting auspicious dates for rituals such as statue construction, ceremonies, or consecration was a crucial task. The {{ill|Mahāvairocanābhisaṃbodhi Sūtra Commentary|ja|大日経疏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mahāvairocanābhisaṃbodhi Sūtra Commentary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by {{ill|Yi Xing|en|Yi Xing|WD=Q710705|lt=Ichigyō|1=Yi Xing}} emphasized the importance of this process as a test of an {{ill|Acharya|en|Acharya|WD=Q1351823|lt=Acharya|1=Acharya}}'s abilities.
Monks such as {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|WD=Q222227|lt=Kūkai|1=Kūkai}}, {{ill|Ennin|en|Ennin|WD=Q732282|lt=Ennin|1=Ennin}}, and {{ill|Enchin|en|Enchin|WD=Q1972505|lt=Enchin|1=Enchin}} brought the {{ill|Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja|宿曜経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 文殊師利菩薩及諸仙所説吉凶時日善悪宿曜経|1=Sūtra on the Astrology of Auspicious and Inauspicious Days and Good and Evil Spoken by Mañjuśrī Bodhisattva and Celestial Beings|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500028}} to Japan, and {{ill|Ninkan|ja|仁観|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} further developed its study. In {{ill|957|en|957|WD=Q23711|lt=957|1=957}} ({{ill|Tiande|en|Tiande|WD=Q10939590|lt=Tento era|1=Tiande}} 1), {{ill|Nichien|ja|日延|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichien|qq=}} brought the {{ill|Futen Calendar|ja|符天暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futen Calendar|qq=}} from {{ill|Wuyue|en|Wuyue|WD=Q1193822|lt=Wuyue|1=Wuyue}}, intensifying research. {{ill|Hōzō (Japanese monk)|ja|法蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōzō (Japanese monk)|1=Q11555339|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555339}} in {{ill|Ōwa|en|Ōwa|WD=Q1324768|lt=Ōwa era|1=Ōwa}} 1 ({{ill|963|en|963|WD=Q23562|lt=963|1=963}}) debated {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|en|Kamo no Yasunori|WD=Q6358935|lt=Kamo no Yasunori|1=Kamo no Yasunori}} over the use of auspicious dates for {{ill|Emperor Murakami|en|Emperor Murakami|WD=Q374015|lt=Emperor Murakami|1=Emperor Murakami}}’s offerings. This marked the formal establishment of Shukuyōdō in Japan.
== Legacy ==
* In the {{ill|The Tale of Genji|en|The Tale of Genji|WD=Q8269|lt=Genji Monogatari|1=The Tale of Genji}}, a scene in the "Kiritsubo" chapter describes {{ill|Hikaru Genji|en|Hikaru Genji|WD=Q997646|lt=Hikaru Genji|1=Hikaru Genji}}’s fate being predicted by a Shukuyō astrologer. * During the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, {{ill|Kujō Yoritsune|en|Kujō Yoritsune|WD=Q471780|lt=Fujiwara no Yoritsune|1=Kujō Yoritsune}} employed a Shukuyō practitioner, {{ill|Chinhyo|ja|珍誉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinhyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, to select auspicious locations for the {{ill|Shogunate|ja|御所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogunate|1=gosho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489051}}.

{{Cite_sources|date=2019-10-07}}
{{nihongo|Shukuyōdō|宿曜道}} is a type of {{ill|Astrology|en|Astrology|WD=Q34362|lt=astrology|1=Astrology}} introduced to {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} by monks studying abroad, including {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|WD=Q222227|lt=Kūkai|1=Kūkai}}, as a branch of {{ill|Mikkyō|en|Mikkyō|WD=Q336102|lt=Esoteric Buddhism|1=Mikkyō}}. It is also known as "Esoteric astrology" or "Shukuyō astrology."
== Overview ==
The practice blends elements of {{ill|Hindu astrology|en|Hindu astrology|WD=Q740253|lt=Indian astrology|1=Hindu astrology}} (a fusion of {{ill|Western astrology|en|Western astrology|WD=Q2266871|lt=Western astrology|1=Western astrology}} and ancient Indian lunar astrology), Taoist celestial deity worship, and {{ill|Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽五行思想}}. It determines auspicious and inauspicious days based on the movements of celestial bodies such as the {{ill|Big Dipper|en|Big Dipper|WD=Q10460|lt=Big Dipper|1=Big Dipper}}, {{ill|Navagraha|en|Navagraha|WD=Q1078632|lt=Navagraha|1=Navagraha}}, {{ill|Zodiac|ja|十二宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zodiac|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サイン (占星術)|1=Astrological sign|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1795024}}, and lunar mansions like the {{ill|Nakshatra|ja|二十七宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakshatra|1=Q11371366|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371366}} or {{ill|Twenty-Eight Mansions|en|Twenty-Eight Mansions|WD=Q1378760|lt=Lunar mansions|1=Twenty-Eight Mansions}}. When an inauspicious day is identified, practitioners would perform rituals to honor the deities associated with those stars to improve fortune.
The foundational scriptures include the {{ill|Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja|宿曜経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 文殊師利菩薩及諸仙所説吉凶時日善悪宿曜経|1=Sūtra on the Astrology of Auspicious and Inauspicious Days and Good and Evil Spoken by Mañjuśrī Bodhisattva and Celestial Beings|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500028}}, {{ill|Brahma Agni Sutra|ja|梵天火羅九曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Brahma Agni Sutra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Shichi Yō Seishin Betsu Gōhō|ja|七曜星辰別行法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi Yō Seishin Betsu Gōhō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. One unique technique is {{ill|Sankū Hikkyū|ja|三九秘宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankū Hikkyū|1=Q11354168|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354168}}, which has a simplified version popularized as "Shukuyō astrology."
In {{ill|Mikkyō|en|Mikkyō|WD=Q336102|lt=Esoteric Buddhism|1=Mikkyō}}, selecting auspicious dates for rituals such as statue construction, ceremonies, or consecration was a crucial task. The {{ill|Mahāvairocanābhisaṃbodhi Sūtra Commentary|ja|大日経疏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mahāvairocanābhisaṃbodhi Sūtra Commentary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by {{ill|Yi Xing|en|Yi Xing|WD=Q710705|lt=Ichigyō|1=Yi Xing}} emphasized the importance of this process as a test of an {{ill|Acharya|en|Acharya|WD=Q1351823|lt=Acharya|1=Acharya}}'s abilities.
Monks such as {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|WD=Q222227|lt=Kūkai|1=Kūkai}}, {{ill|Ennin|en|Ennin|WD=Q732282|lt=Ennin|1=Ennin}}, and {{ill|Enchin|en|Enchin|WD=Q1972505|lt=Enchin|1=Enchin}} brought the {{ill|Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja|宿曜経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sutra of Sukuyokyo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 文殊師利菩薩及諸仙所説吉凶時日善悪宿曜経|1=Sūtra on the Astrology of Auspicious and Inauspicious Days and Good and Evil Spoken by Mañjuśrī Bodhisattva and Celestial Beings|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500028}} to Japan, and {{ill|Ninkan|ja|仁観|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} further developed its study. In {{ill|957|en|957|WD=Q23711|lt=957|1=957}} ({{ill|Tiande|en|Tiande|WD=Q10939590|lt=Tento era|1=Tiande}} 1), {{ill|Nichien|ja|日延|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichien|qq=}} brought the {{ill|Futen Calendar|ja|符天暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futen Calendar|qq=}} from {{ill|Wuyue|en|Wuyue|WD=Q1193822|lt=Wuyue|1=Wuyue}}, intensifying research. {{ill|Hōzō (Japanese monk)|ja|法蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōzō (Japanese monk)|1=Q11555339|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555339}} in {{ill|Ōwa|en|Ōwa|WD=Q1324768|lt=Ōwa era|1=Ōwa}} 1 ({{ill|963|en|963|WD=Q23562|lt=963|1=963}}) debated {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|en|Kamo no Yasunori|WD=Q6358935|lt=Kamo no Yasunori|1=Kamo no Yasunori}} over the use of auspicious dates for {{ill|Emperor Murakami|en|Emperor Murakami|WD=Q374015|lt=Emperor Murakami|1=Emperor Murakami}}’s offerings. This marked the formal establishment of Shukuyōdō in Japan.

* In the {{ill|The Tale of Genji|en|The Tale of Genji|WD=Q8269|lt=Genji Monogatari|1=The Tale of Genji}}, a scene in the "Kiritsubo" chapter describes {{ill|Hikaru Genji|en|Hikaru Genji|WD=Q997646|lt=Hikaru Genji|1=Hikaru Genji}}’s fate being predicted by a Shukuyō astrologer. * During the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, {{ill|Kujō Yoritsune|en|Kujō Yoritsune|WD=Q471780|lt=Fujiwara no Yoritsune|1=Kujō Yoritsune}} employed a Shukuyō practitioner, {{ill|Chinhyo|ja|珍誉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinhyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, to select auspicious locations for the {{ill|Shogunate|ja|御所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogunate|1=gosho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489051}}.
